title: Three Squares
description: There are N distinct points located at integer coordinates in the plane. We want to place three identical axis-parallel squares on the plane, such that each of the N points is located inside (or on the borders of) at least one of the squares. What is the minimum possible side length of the squares?
input: The first line contains the integer number N (1 ≤ N ≤ 100000). The next N lines contain two space-separated integers each, the x and y coordinates of one of the points (0 ≤ x, y ≤ 109 ).
output: Print the minimum possible side length of the 3 squares, such that all the N points are covered by them.
hint: You can place the 3 squares with their bottom-left corners at the following coordinates: (0,0), (8,0), (7,1).
source: ICPC, Regionals, Europe, Southeastern European Regional Contest, SEERC 2016
